facts.details: Gemini Code Assist (formerly Duet AI) supports a wide range of environments: VS Code (via extension), JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, GoLand, WebStorm, CLion, DataGrip, PhpStorm, Rider, RubyMine), Android Studio, Google Cloud Shell Editor (pre-installed), and Google Cloud Workstations.

Gemini 2.5 Flash and Pro models feature a 1M token context window. The upcoming Gemini 3 Pro (in preview) extends this to 2M tokens. This allows the assistant to process entire codebases, large documentation sets, or long execution logs in a single request.

Gemini CLI is an open-source AI agent that provides terminal-based code understanding, file manipulation, and command execution. In IDEs, 'Agent Mode' (preview) allows the assistant to execute terminal commands, perform multi-step reasoning, and handle multi-file refactors autonomously using tools and MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers.

Supports 'Local Codebase Awareness' which indexes the local project to provide context-aware suggestions. Enterprise users can also index private remote repositories. It respects .aiexclude and .gitignore files to manage indexing scope.

Supports 'Rules' (User or Workspace level) for defining coding styles and preferences. 'Custom Commands' (e.g., /add-comments) can be created in IDE settings. For project-wide instructions, Agent Mode and Gemini CLI support repo-root instruction files, similar to CLAUDE.md.
Gemini offers a significantly larger context window (1M vs ~128k) and deeper Google Cloud/Firebase integration compared to GitHub Copilot.
Cursor is a full IDE fork with superior multi-file 'Composer' editing, while Gemini relies on Agent Mode and long context.

facts.category: Comparison with Competitors - Claude Code CLI
facts.details: Both offer agentic CLI capabilities, but Gemini provides a larger context window (1M vs 200k) and higher free tier limits (180k completions/month) compared to Claude Code CLI.

recommendations: Adopt a repo-root instruction file to standardize instructions for both Gemini CLI and IDE Agent Mode.
Utilize Agent Mode (Insiders channel) for complex multi-file refactoring tasks that exceed standard chat capabilities.
Configure .aiexclude to prevent sensitive or irrelevant files from consuming the 1M token context window during indexing.
Integrate MCP servers to extend Gemini's capabilities with external tools (e.g., database querying, specialized API docs).
unresolved_questions: Exact release date for Gemini 3 Pro's 2M token window in the stable channel.
Whether Gemini Code Assist will eventually support CLAUDE.md natively for cross-tool compatibility without manual renaming.
Performance impact of 1M+ token context on response latency compared to RAG-based approaches like Cursor.
